Case Narrative

In brief, for the investigating officer:

  • A total of Rs 2,413,191.33 was moved across the accounts in this case, with Rs 914,995.00 coming from outside the suspect network.
  • Pushpendra Pathak sent out Rs 1,363,955.33, making it the largest source of funds.
  • Pushpendra Pathak spread money to 271 different accounts.
  • Pushpendra Pathak collected money from 76 different accounts.
  • Rs 36,148,861.00 of incoming funds was used for onward transfers or withdrawals, and Rs 205,099.00 could not be fully traced.
  • The account PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION is the first to investigate due to its suspicious behaviors.

4 · Findings by Account

PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION strong
Account Number 18306700003 · IDFC First Bank · Score 759
Rank #1

The account PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION (18306700003) exhibits suspicious money behavior, characterized by a large credit of 2000000.00 and subsequent debit transactions. This pattern, combined with other findings, raises concerns about potential money laundering or structuring activities. The account's transactions suggest a deliberate attempt to evade detection.

  • Money Trail Tracing strong — A credit of 2000000.00 was traced into 6 subsequent debit transactions, with the entire amount allocated and none remaining untraced, indicating a potential money laundering scheme. (+5 more of this type)
  • Structuring Smurfing Detection strong — The account made 35 debit transactions on 2025-04-03, each below the individual cutoff, but totalling 8455432.00, suggesting structuring to evade detection. (+2 more of this type)
  • Failed Reversed Transaction Detection weak — A debit of 500000.0 was followed by a credit of 500000.0 on the same day, indicating a possible failed reversed transaction or erroneous transaction correction.
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph with 378 nodes and 812 directed transaction edges was constructed, revealing complex transaction patterns involving multiple accounts.
  • Reversal Clusters weak — The account has 5 debit-reversal pairs, indicating systematic transaction reversal activity, which may be used to conceal illicit transactions.
  • Round Value Debit Patterns weak — The account made 45 suspicious round-value debits totalling 13396000.00, with repeated amounts, suggesting a potential pattern of money laundering or structuring.
Assessment: 6 strong pattern type(s) and 5 distinct pattern(s) — strong evidence.

Data Security & Method (Appendix)

What stays on this machine

  • All fraud detection, scoring and ranking run on this machine (deterministic code).
  • Raw bank statements and extracted transactions never leave the machine.
  • The money-flow graph, clusters and suspicion scores are computed locally.

What is sent to an external service

  • Only small, redacted text samples are sent to the language model to describe a statement's column layout when local parsing is insufficient.
  • For images / scanned PDFs, the page pixels are sent to a vision model (the one unavoidable case).
  • The language model only rewrites explanations into plain language; it never changes a number, account, or decision.
